title: SACRAMENTO, CA
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 12 bans landlords in California from charging exorbitant security deposits.
California now limits security deposits to only one month's rent.
Massive security deposits create insurmountable barriers to housing affordability and accessibility for millions of Californians.
The laws on ensuring affordable security deposits in California haven't changed substantially since the 1970s.
53% of California renters indicate that they cannot get an apartment because they can't afford to pay two months' rent as a security deposit.
Expensive security deposits are often imposed on immigrants and people of color in California.
Capping high security deposits advances a measure of equity for immigrants and people of color in California.
